text: In this case, we are using a forearm approach with a DaVinci XI platform using a zero-degree optic.
The surgery starts as a standard left lung bridge section for lung cancer treatment, opening the inferior pulmonary ligament, extracting stations 8 and 9, and dissecting lower pulmonary vein.
It's important to open the posterior mediastinum to acquire more space to dissect the vein.
Since it's a sublubary section, the vein section should go through the parenchyma and the segmental branches should be exposed.
For S10 segmentectomy, we dissect between the V6 and basilar vein, where we can find the basilar bronchus.
The dissection is important to create a space for posterior division of the S6 from the S10.
That space will be noticed when we dissect the interlobar artery from the interlobar fissure a few steps ahead.
We continue with the posterior mediastinum dissection with station 7 lymphadenectomy and stations 10 and 11.
After that, we go up to resect stations 5 and 6 and complete the N2 station lymphadenectomy.
So, we start with the interlobar fissure exploration.
The interlobar artery is identified and the dissection of lymph node 11 is performed.
At this point, we find our previous dissection between the S6 and S10.
It facilitates the division of S10 upper boundary with S6 parenchyma.
With the parenchyma division, we can clearly see the S10 Euler structures, separated from S6.
We continue with the dissection of intersegmental stations to facilitate the identification of A10.
The 3D reconstruction is very useful to correct identification of Euler structures and it becoming indispensable for complex sublobal resections.
The artery is divided and the segmental bronchus appears right behind it.
The B10 is dissected from the anterior face of V10 and then is divided with a stapler.
We do not perform selective ventilation tests by routing, since the 3D reconstruction and the wide section provide a secure view of all branches, leaving no doubt about their identities.
Doing that, the V10 is completely exposed and ready to be stapled.
To define the intersegmental plane between S10 and S9, we use the ICG and Firefly.
Since all the S10 Euler structures were treated, it's notable the difference between the vascularized parenchyma and the one that will be resected.
Finally we extract the specimen, insert the chest tube and confirm the lung expansion.
